_notes: W4A-8 (DQ1/DQ3, D7): per-feed dependency class + PREWRITTEN stale policy.
Gated by CHAD_DQ_POLICIES (off|shadow|enforce, default off). Sentinel
keeps sole ownership of config/exterminator.json; this file is the
reader-side policy the P18 gaps were missing.
Policy vocabulary (all with existing production archetypes, PLAN_W4A P17):
block_entries  - stale/corrupt/missing => refuse NEW entries; reduce-only
/ exits still allowed (margin_block precedent, the XOV2
degrade_loud   - documented fallback + marker + alert; behavior otherwise
hold_last_loud - retain the last-applied decision, loud (LC5 §5.4).
ignore         - advisory feeds; today's fail-open, now DECLARED not
The artifact's own ttl_seconds WINS over the value here (house rule); the
config value is the fallback + the citation of record.
INITIAL WIRED SITES (D7): exactly the two P18 gaps -- scr_state (SCR gate,
fail-open -> fail-closed under enforce) and the stop-bus inputs
(ibkr_status / pnl_state, DQ_INPUT_DEAD loudness). Every other row here is
config + shadow evidence only until a later consolidation.

runtime/scr_state.json._note: P18: the SCR hard-block gate reads scr_state.json raw, no ts/ttl check, fail-open -- a frozen PAUSED blocks forever and a corrupt file silently disables the governor. Under DQ enforce, stale/corrupt/missing => block NEW entries (exits free), making a dead SCR loud instead of silently disarming.

runtime/ibkr_status.json._note: P18: a dead ibkr_status.json silently disables the stop-bus latency halt. Under DQ enforce the DQ_INPUT_DEAD marker + alert fire; behavior change beyond loudness: none in W4A.

runtime/drawdown_state.json.wired_site: handled natively by LC5 §5.4 (compute_lc5_state); DQ row is declarative

runtime/regime_state.json.wired_site: handled natively by the fuse regime stamp (fail-unknown); DQ row is declarative
